name: Bill Holman
abstract: Willis Leonard Holman (May 21, 1927 – May 6, 2024) was an American composer, arranger, conductor, saxophonist, and songwriter working in jazz and traditional pop. His career spanned over seven decades, starting with the Charlie Barnet orchestra in 1950.

interviews.title: Bill Holman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: I played in Bill Holman’s band for five years so I look at his charts
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Pell
about_quotes.evidence: I went to Bill Holman and said, “let’s do ‘Supersax’ but with Lester.” ... Bill Holman and I decided let’s do a vocal album ... The charts that Bill Holman made as background with the Prez sound
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Pell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Snader Telescription in which the title number is played by Charlie Barnet and His Orchestra, including Claude Williamson, piano, Bill Holman, tenor sax...

about_quotes.speaker: Don Menza and Sam Noto
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Bill Holman as an arranger Stan Kenton hired ('The Bill Holmans...') and later cites him as an early arranger whose writing style the interviewee admired ('early on were Bill Holman, I loved that linear writing that he did'). There is no evidence of direct personal interacti
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Menza and Sam Noto interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: So we had all the music written for us. Bill Holman, Ernie Wilkins, Quincy, Bill Russo. So we had tons, all this ©Fillius Jazz Archive -5- original, and I started writing for the band at one point.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: And Bill Holman came in with music that could reach from here down – we’re on the eighth floor. And he conducted. But he came in with them killers. And the band just played it...
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001
